note: Mathlib/RingTheory/LaurentSeries.lean:102 — `abbrev LaurentSeries (R) [Zero R] := R⟦ℤ⟧`, docstring: "LaurentSeries R is the type of formal Laurent series with coefficients in R, denoted R⸨X⸩"; this is the concept's direct definition.

Mathlib/Analysis/SpecificLimits/Normed.lean:776 — docstring literally "The alternating series test" for antitone sequences tending to 0; this theorem (Leibniz test) is the central content of the Wikipedia article on alternating series.

Mathlib/NumberTheory/GaussSum.lean:72 — `def gaussSum (χ : MulChar R R') (ψ : AddChar R R') : R' := ∑ a, χ a * ψ a`; Gauss sums are a classical named instance of exponential sums (character sums), not the general concept itself — no general exponential-sum definition exists in Mathlib.
